page: Amazon Web Services g4ad.2xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7R32 processor. It features 8 vCPUs (4 physical cores with 2 threads per core) running at 3.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, supported by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. The instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. For graphics and parallel computing workloads, it includes a single AMD Radeon Pro Navi V520 GPU with 8 GB of dedicated VRAM. Local storage is provided via a 300 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume, and baseline network bandwidth is rated at 4 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals a mixed performance profile. The instance demonstrates average performance in Geekbench single-core and multi-core tests, as well as Passmark CPU and memory evaluations. However, raw compute benchmarks show limitations, with stress-ng single-core performance falling into the bottom 10% and multi-core performance in the bottom 25%. Large-block memory bandwidth read and write speeds are also weak, landing in the bottom 25%. Conversely, small-block cached memory reads and single-threaded gzip compression perform at average levels. For machine learning, LLM inference on a small 135M model achieves average speeds, but performance drops significantly to weak or poor levels when processing a medium 7B model.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the g4ad.2xlarge offers a budget-friendly entry point for GPU-accelerated workloads by utilizing AMD Radeon hardware rather than more expensive alternatives. The inclusion of local NVMe SSD storage and dedicated CPU cores provides reliable resource density for its tier. This instance is best suited for graphics-intensive applications, game streaming, virtual workstations, and moderate database operations or web serving that can leverage GPU acceleration alongside local high-speed storage, while avoiding heavy CPU-bound or large-scale LLM inference tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services g4ad.2xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance featuring an AMD EPYC 7R32 processor with 8 vCPUs, 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory, and a dedicated AMD Radeon Pro Navi V520 GPU with 8 GB of VRAM. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed by the Nitro hypervisor, it includes a 300 GB local NVMe SSD and a 4 Gbps baseline network interface. Performance benchmarks indicate average capabilities in general integer and memory operations, though raw CPU-intensive tasks and large-block memory bandwidth show weaker results. The instance offers a cost-effective option for graphics-intensive workloads, virtual desktops, and moderate database tasks, balancing dedicated hardware resources with local high-speed storage, while being less suited for heavy CPU-bound computations or large language model inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services g4ad.2xlarge is an x86_64 instance featuring 8 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, a 300 GB NVMe SSD, and an AMD Radeon Pro Navi V520 GPU with 8 GB VRAM for graphics-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services g4ad.2xlarge features 8 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, a 300 GB NVMe SSD, and an AMD Radeon Pro Navi V520 GPU for graphics workloads.
tagline: A GPU-accelerated instance featuring AMD Radeon graphics, dedicated EPYC processors, and local NVMe storage for graphics-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an 8-vCPU AMD EPYC 7R32 processor and 32 GB DDR4 RAM
Equipped with one AMD Radeon Pro Navi V520 GPU with 8 GB VRAM
Includes 300 GB of local NVMe SSD storage for low-latency I/O
Delivers average performance in standard database and web serving benchmarks
Best suited for virtual workstations, graphics rendering, and game streaming
